title: Incorrect count(*) when partial indices exist
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value FLOAT, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE INDEX t2_value_index ON t2(value) WHERE value > 2.0;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t2 WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
INSERT INTO t2(name, value) SELECT 'D', NULL UNION SELECT 'E', 4.5;
key_bug_features: count(*) optimization ignores partial indices in queries
